entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Your form is excellent, Edren. Cael taught you the
high guard, I assume. He was always precise.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Torren, you favour your left side when tired. The
spirits could have told you that. Did you not ask?

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Lira — how does it feel to defend a kingdom that
never deserved your loyalty? I'm genuinely curious.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Sable. You carry no titles, no magic, no ancestral
weapon. Just stubbornness. I've seen empires fall to less.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Maren. You've read about me, haven't you? Tell me
— did the accounts do me justice?

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Ah. That's new. In eight hundred years, no one has
done that before.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Your grief makes you legible. Every wound tells me
exactly how to hurt you next.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Another age. Another failure. The cycle is
exquisitely predictable.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Interesting. You actually drew blood. When did you
last sharpen that blade, Edren? It's good work.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Clever. The Pallor's defences are not infinite.
Neither, I should note, is your time.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
You are the first to push me this far in eight
centuries. I want you to understand what that means. It means
nothing has changed. It just took longer this time.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Time is a courtesy I extend to mortals. I'm
withdrawing it.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
You should have stopped. Everyone stops. Every age,
every cycle — they fight until they understand, and then they
stop. That's not weakness. That's wisdom.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
That weapon is borrowed time, child. He's already
gone. What you're swinging is grief shaped like a sword.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Feel that? That's not an attack. That's the truth.
The Pallor doesn't create despair. It reveals what was already
there.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
I was the first to understand the Pallor. The first
to name it. And the first to realize that naming it changes
nothing. Understanding is not a weapon. It is a weight.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
You will reach the Convergence. You will face what
I faced. And you will make the same choice I made. Everyone does.

entries.speaker: vaelith
lines:
Something changed. I can feel it. The ley network
should be dead by now. It's not. You did something none of them
did. I just... don't understand what.
